articles.title: Transgender women athletes banned from female Olympic events
articles.description:
The IOC agreed to a new eligibility policy aligning with Trump's executive order on sports ahead of the 2028 Los Angeles Games.

The post Transgender women athletes banned from female Olympic events appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: IOC bans transgender women from female Olympic events starting 2028.
Policy aligns with Trump's executive order affecting all Olympic sports.
Boston athletes may face new challenges in competition and inclusion.

articles.title: Councilors, Pressley doubling down on demands for bodycam footage after deadly police shooting
articles.description:
The vote comes just weeks after the fatal police shooting in Roxbury yielded a controversial manslaughter charge for the officer involved.

The post Councilors, Pressley doubling down on demands for bodycam footage after deadly police shooting appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Councilors and Rep. Ayanna Pressley demand bodycam footage from Roxbury's recent police shooting.
The shooting resulted in a controversial manslaughter charge against the involved officer.
Residents seek transparency and accountability in police actions following the incident.

articles.title: New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster to open at Six Flags
articles.description:
The much-anticipated Quantum Accelerator opens on April 11.

The post New England’s only dual-launch straddle coaster to open at Six Flags appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Quantum Accelerator, New England's only dual-launch straddle coaster, opens at Six Flags on April 11.
The coaster aims to attract more visitors to the park, boosting local tourism and economy.
Residents can expect increased traffic and activity in the area surrounding Six Flags.

articles.title: A new program will make Boston the 1st major-city school district to require AI training
articles.description:
The program aims to ensure high school graduates are proficient in AI to advance their careers, Mayor Michelle Wu says.

The post A new program will make Boston the 1st major-city school district to require AI training appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston becomes the first major city to mandate AI training in schools.
Mayor Michelle Wu announced the program to enhance high school graduates' career readiness.
The initiative aims to equip students with essential AI skills for future job markets.

articles.title: After mix-up, Matthew Farwell’s lawyers amend court filing, say unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore’s unborn child
articles.description:
Attorneys blamed a reporter for misreading a footnote and incorrectly claiming that Farwell's brother was the father of Birchmore's child.

The post After mix-up, Matthew Farwell’s lawyers amend court filing, say unnamed person fathered Sandra Birchmore’s unborn child appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Matthew Farwell's lawyers corrected a court filing regarding Sandra Birchmore's unborn child's paternity.
Attorneys clarified that an unnamed person, not Farwell's brother, is the child's father.
This clarification may affect ongoing legal proceedings and public perception in Boston.

articles.title: A Boston student runs the Marathon to honor her best friend — and the father she lost
articles.description:
The 21-year-old Boston runner is honoring a former Marathoner’s legacy while supporting trauma research through Brigham and Women’s Stepping Strong team.

The post A Boston student runs the Marathon to honor her best friend — and the father she lost appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston student Lily Penzone, 21, runs Marathon to honor her late father and best friend.
Penzone supports trauma research through Brigham and Women’s Stepping Strong team during the event.
Her efforts raise awareness and funds for trauma research, benefiting Boston's medical community.
